title: Digital Marketing Projects
text:
This page showcases selected projects from my professional and academic experience in campaign strategy, content creation, and lifecycle marketing.



📅 March 2026 – Present | Pasadena, CA
As Digital Marketing Assistant, I create and manage content across social media, email campaigns, and video — supporting the RBAC’s mission of water safety, community engagement, and program awareness.


I designed and distributed monthly employee newsletters using Feathr, covering staff spotlights, upcoming events, new hires, and internal announcements.



Highlighted Gratitude Grams, welcomed new team members, announced CPR/AED certification opportunities, and introduced my own role as Digital Marketing Assistant.

✨ View March Newsletter




Promoted the Dodgers Night employee event with RSVP link, introduced new team members, highlighted the Pasadena Triathlon and UCLA Spring Game, and teased the upcoming Mother’s Day Scavenger Hunt.

✨ View April Newsletter







Featured in an RBAC community-wide email blast, this video stars me as Assistant Lifeguard Manager, walking the RBAC community through the daily pool preparation checklist — showing how water safety is built into everything before a swimmer ever steps on deck.


Sent to the full RBAC community as part of Water Safety Month (May 2026), promoting the Treading for Water Safety Challenge fundraiser.

 







📧 View the Community Email Blast





I create regular posts for @theRBAC covering program promotions, water safety awareness, community events, and staff highlights.

text:

text:

Rose Bowl Stadium Guest Service Representative | Pasadena, CA | April 2025 – Present

Provide guest services, resolve issues, and support VIP access during major events.
Assist Ticketmaster entry operations including scanning, credentials, and wrist banding
Support event managers to maintain smooth operations for high‑volume crowds Major Events: FIFA World Cup (50,000+), Rose Bowl Game (90,000+)

Havas Street Brand Ambassador | Los Angeles, CA | November 2025

Supported event activation, guest engagement, and brand representation at high‑traffic events for Farmer John Mobile Tour and RunDisney X Brooks

Create and manage digital content for social media, email campaigns, and website updates
Analyze campaign performance and audience engagement to optimize marketing strategies

Human Resources Assistant | Pasadena, CA | April 2024 - Present

Lead talent acquisition and onboarding for 100+ employees, ensuring accurate documentation and smooth processes
Manage confidential employee records, HR inboxes, and internal communications
Support payroll and timekeeping through Paychex Flex
Coordinate scheduling, invoices, and administrative workflows for director

Assistant Lifeguard Manager | Pasadena, CA | December 2024 - Present

Supervise 50+ staff and manage daily schedules, adjusting coverage in real time
Lead interviews, orientations, and training for new hires.
Support operations including inventory, customer service, and staff meetings.





MS Digital Marketing – Cal Poly Pomona

Bilingual: English (Fluent), Spanish (Conversational)

title: Marketing Analytics Projects
text:
This page showcases my hands-on experience using data analytics, visualization, and strategic insight to solve real-world marketing problems.



Culminating Experience Project (CEP) — Graduate research investigating whether AI-personalized emails generate higher consumer trust compared to standard emails, using an experimental survey design.

🎯 Focus: AI email personalization, consumer trust, and email marketing effectiveness
📊 Tools Used: R, Qualtrics, Excel
🔬 Methodology: Experimental design (A/B test), One-Way ANOVA, Tukey HSD post-hoc test, Likert-scale survey (10 trust items)
📈 Outcome: AI-personalized emails scored slightly higher in trust (M = 4.24) versus control (M = 4.16), but the difference was not statistically significant (F = 0.745, p = 0.39), suggesting trust levels are comparably high across both conditions.




Average Trust Score by Condition



Bar chart showing AI condition (4.24) vs Control condition (4.16) average trust scores


95% Tukey Confidence Interval



Tukey confidence interval plot showing the difference in means crosses zero, indicating no significant difference


Summary Statistics Table







Statistic
Value




Mean Trust (AI)
4.24


Mean Trust (Control)
4.16


Mean Difference
0.08


F-Statistic
0.745


p-Value
0.39


95% CI (Tukey)
[-0.280, 0.110]


Tukey p-Value
0.39


Interpretation
No statistically significant difference in trust



Trust Item Breakdown by Condition



Horizontal bar chart comparing AI vs Control scores across all 10 trust survey items







Both AI and control emails generated high trust scores (above 4.0 on a 5-point scale), suggesting the email content itself was well-received regardless of personalization.
The AI condition scored higher on data comfort and accuracy/reliability, while the control scored slightly higher on privacy respect and authenticity.
The lack of statistical significance may reflect a ceiling effect — participants rated both emails highly, leaving little room to detect meaningful differences.
Future research could explore trust differences with larger, more diverse samples or test emails with stronger AI personalization cues.
